front: How does the branched-chain amino acid pathway relate to maple syrup urine disease?
back: Branched-chain aminotransferase (BCAT) and the BCKDH complex catabolize Leu, Ile, Val. BCKDH deficiency causes maple syrup urine disease with accumulation of BCAAs and their ketoacids. Symptoms include maple syrup odor, encephalopathy, and intellectual disability.
